name: IDFC FIRST Millennia Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Annual fee waived on spending ₹1,50,000 or more in the previous year

categoryRates.description: 1 Reward Point per ₹100 spent on all purchases
6X Reward Points (6 RP per ₹100) on online transactions
3X Reward Points (3 RP per ₹100) on dining transactions

cappedCategories.description: 6X rewards on online spends capped at 5,000 bonus RP per month

welcomeBenefits.description: 1,000 bonus Reward Points on card activation
welcomeBenefits.condition: On payment of joining fee and first transaction

milestoneBenefits.benefit: Annual fee waiver + 2,000 bonus Reward Points

additionalBenefits: 6X rewards on online shopping including Amazon, Flipkart, etc.
4 complimentary domestic lounge visits per year
Zero interest on ATM cash withdrawals for up to 48 days
pros: Strong 6X rewards on online shopping covers most e-commerce spends
Never-expiring reward points unlike most competing cards
Easy fee waiver at ₹1.5L annual spend
Zero interest on ATM cash withdrawals is a unique feature
4 domestic lounge visits at a reasonable price point
cons: Low base earn rate of 1 RP per ₹100 (0.25% return)
Point value of ₹0.25 is below average
3.5% forex markup makes it unsuitable for international use
idealFor: Heavy online shoppers who want accelerated rewards on e-commerce
Users who value never-expiring reward points
Budget-conscious users looking for a mid-tier card with easy fee waiver

faqs.question: What counts as online spends for 6X rewards?
faqs.answer: Online spends include transactions on e-commerce platforms like Amazon, Flipkart, Myntra, and other online merchants. App-based purchases and online subscriptions also qualify. Wallet loading and rent payments are excluded.

faqs.question: Do IDFC FIRST reward points expire?
faqs.answer: No, IDFC FIRST Bank reward points never expire as long as your credit card account remains active. This is a standout benefit compared to most banks that expire points in 2-3 years.

faqs.question: How does zero interest ATM withdrawal work?
faqs.answer: You can withdraw cash from ATMs using your IDFC FIRST Millennia card and pay zero interest if you repay within the billing cycle (up to 48 days). Most other cards charge 2.5-3.5% per month on cash advances.

faqs.question: Is this card better than the IDFC FIRST Wealth?
faqs.answer: The Millennia offers 6X on online and 3X on dining but only 1X base, while the Wealth gives flat 6X on everything. If your spends are concentrated online, Millennia matches Wealth. For diverse spending, Wealth is better.
